Marcela Carena.name: Marcela Carena
Marcela Carena.role: Research Faculty Office of Executive Leadership
Marcela Carena.summary: Marcela Carena is an Executive Director, Research Faculty Office of Executive Leadership. Their research is connected to Particle Physics. Her research explores the possible connections between the Higgs boson, dark matter, and the origin of matter in the universe. In 2010, Carena won the Research Award from the Alexander von Humboldt Foundation, and she was a Simons Distinguished Scholar at the Kavli Institute in Santa Barbara in 2013.

Freddy Cachazo.name: Freddy Cachazo
Freddy Cachazo.role: Research Faculty Office of Executive Leadership
Freddy Cachazo.summary: Freddy Cachazo is a Deputy Director, Research Faculty Office of Executive Leadership. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics and Quantum Fields and Strings. Freddy Cachazo (PhD Harvard University, 2002) has been a faculty member at Perimeter Institute since 2005. Cachazo is one of the world's leading experts in the study and computation of scattering amplitudes in gauge theories, such as quantum chromodynamics and N=4 super Yang-Mills, and in Einstein's gravity theory.

Bianca Dittrich.name: Bianca Dittrich
Bianca Dittrich.role: Research Faculty Office of Executive Leadership
Bianca Dittrich.summary: Bianca Dittrich is Faculty Chair, Research Faculty Office of Executive Leadership. Her research focuses on quantum gravity and the problem of understanding the nature of space and time, contributing to models of quantum spacetime.

Robert Myers.name: Robert Myers
Robert Myers.secondary_position: BMO Financial Group Isaac Newton Chair in Theoretical Physics
Robert Myers.summary: Robert Myers is a Director Emeritus, Research Faculty Perimeter Research Chair. Their research is connected to Quantum Fields and Strings. at Princeton University in 1986, after which he was a postdoctoral researcher at what became the Kavli Institute for Theoretical Physics at the University of California, Santa Barbara. Professor Myers was awarded the Herzberg Medal in 1999 by the Canadian Association of Physicists (CAP) for seminal contributions to our understanding of black hole microphysics and D-branes.

Asimina Arvanitaki.name: Asimina Arvanitaki
Asimina Arvanitaki.position: Stavros Niarchos Foundation Aristarchus Chair in Theoretical Physics
Asimina Arvanitaki.summary: Asimina Arvanitaki is a Stavros Niarchos Foundation Aristarchus Chair in Theoretical Physics, Research Faculty Perimeter Research Chair. Their research is connected to Particle Physics. Asimina Arvanitaki's research is drawing together ideas from these developments to propose novel theoretical ideas to such longstanding problems, and to build bridges to experimentalists, combining ingenuity with new technologies to develop experimental tests that can probe otherwise-inaccessible phenomena.

Michael Borinsky.name: Michael Borinsky
Michael Borinsky.summary: Michael Borinsky is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics.

Kevin Costello.name: Kevin Costello
Kevin Costello.position: Krembil William Rowan Hamilton Chair in Theoretical Physics
Kevin Costello.summary: Kevin Costello is a Krembil William Rowan Hamilton Chair in Theoretical Physics, Research Faculty Perimeter Research Chair. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics. Kevin Costello's research uses tools from mathematics to explore string theory and quantum field theory. The long-term goal of Kevin Costello's research is to provide rigorous constructions of (parts of) string theory with a view towards formulating string dualities in mathematical terms.

Neal Dalal.name: Neal Dalal
Neal Dalal.summary: Neal Dalal is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Cosmology. Neal Dalal's research interests are primarily in the area of cosmology, including large-scale structure, dark matter, dark energy, and gravitational lensing.

William East.name: William East
William East.summary: William East is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Cosmology and Strong Gravity. William East works at the interface of gravitational physics, astrophysics, and cosmology. William East's research is primarily focused on understanding strong field gravity, using the tools of numerical relativity.

Dominic Else.name: Dominic Else
Dominic Else.summary: Dominic Else is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Matter. Dominic Else is generally interested in topological and non-perturbative approaches to quantum many-body systems. In the past Dominic Else has been deeply interested in gapped topological phases of matter.

Laurent Freidel.name: Laurent Freidel
Laurent Freidel.summary: Laurent Freidel is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics and Quantum Gravity. He has authored or co-authored around 140 publications, many open new research areas and which are known among fellow researchers for combining creative ideas and offering complete, thorough and mathematically detailed arguments. He is currently developing local holography, a new bottom-up approach for quantum gravity that focuses on local corner symmetries that appear in the study of gravitational subsystems.

Davide Gaiotto.name: Davide Gaiotto
Davide Gaiotto.position: Krembil Galileo Galilei Chair in Theoretical Physics
Davide Gaiotto.summary: Davide Gaiotto is a Krembil Galileo Galilei Chair in Theoretical Physics, Research Faculty Perimeter Research Chair. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics and Quantum Fields and Strings. Davide Gaiotto's research focuses on theoretical and mathematical physics, with a particular emphasis on the interplay between quantum field theory (QFT), string theory, algebra and geometry.

Jaume Gomis.name: Jaume Gomis
Jaume Gomis.summary: Jaume Gomis is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics and Quantum Fields and Strings. Jaume Gomis's research focuses on the nonperturbative dynamics of gauge theories and string theory, uncovering the physics and mathematics underlying dualities in gauge theories and string theory and nonperturbative, holographic formulations of quantum gravity.

Lucien Hardy.name: Lucien Hardy
Lucien Hardy.role: Research Faculty Office of Executive Leadership
Lucien Hardy.summary: Lucien Hardy is a Deputy Faculty Chair, Research Faculty Office of Executive Leadership. Their research is connected to Quantum Foundations. He has held research and lecturing positions in various cities across Europe. Recently he has been interested in Time Symmetry in operational theories (and Quantum Theory in Particular).

Yin-Chen He.name: Yin-Chen He
Yin-Chen He.summary: Yin-Chen He is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Matter. Yin-Chen He is interested in the condensed matter theory of strongly correlated system, particularly quantum spin liquid, quantum criticality, conformal field theory, topological phases of matter, quantum field theory and numerical simulations.

Timothy Hsieh.name: Timothy Hsieh
Timothy Hsieh.summary: Timothy Hsieh is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Information and Quantum Matter. Timothy Hsieh is interested in quantum dynamics, applications of synthetic quantum systems for quantum simulation, and topological phases of matter.

Junwu Huang.name: Junwu Huang
Junwu Huang.summary: Junwu Huang is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Cosmology and Particle Physics. Junwu Huang is primarily interested in understanding what lies beyond the Standard Model of particle physics. Junwu Huang is interested in pursuing novel, low-energy experiments for weakly-coupled particles that may make use of small-scale experimental techniques.

Luis Lehner.name: Luis Lehner
Luis Lehner.position: Carlo Fidani Rainer Weiss Chair in Theoretical Physics
Luis Lehner.summary: Luis Lehner is a Carlo Fidani Rainer Weiss Chair in Theoretical Physics, Research Faculty Perimeter Research Chair. Their research is connected to Strong Gravity and Other. Understanding gravity in strong/highly dynamical regimes. Understanding gravitating systems in higher dimensional scenarios.

Alex May.name: Alex May
Alex May.position: Murray Gell-Mann Chair in Theoretical Physics
Alex May.summary: Alex May is a Murray Gell-Mann Chair in Theoretical Physics, Research Faculty Perimeter Research Chair. Their research is connected to Quantum Information. Alex May is interested broadly in quantum information and its applications to physics. Alex May is interested in teaching at other schools and events.

Sabrina Pasterski.name: Sabrina Pasterski
Sabrina Pasterski.summary: Sabrina Pasterski is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Fields and Strings. Her research prior to joining PI includes discovering infinite dimensional symmetry enhancements of the S-matrix, a new observable memory effect in gravity, and a framework for generalizing these features of infrared physics to other theories. As the founder and principal investigator of our new Celestial Holography Initiative, she is leading a team of amplitudes, mathematical physics, and quantum gravity researchers.

Kendrick Smith.name: Kendrick Smith
Kendrick Smith.position: The Daniel Family James Peebles Chair in Theoretical Physics
Kendrick Smith.summary: Kendrick Smith is a The Daniel Family James Peebles Chair in Theoretical Physics, Research Faculty Perimeter Research Chair. Their research is connected to Cosmology. Most recently, Kendrick Smith has joined CHIME, a new experiment in British Columbia which aims to measure the radio sky to orders of magnitude better sensitivity than previous all-sky surveys, with a wide variety of applications. It is the first new Canadian research telescope in several decades.

Lee Smolin.name: Lee Smolin
Lee Smolin.summary: Lee Smolin is Senior Faculty (on leave), Research Faculty. His research is connected to cosmology, quantum foundations, and quantum gravity.

Robert Spekkens.name: Robert Spekkens
Robert Spekkens.summary: Robert Spekkens is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Foundations. His research is focused upon identifying the conceptual innovations that distinguish quantum theories from classical theories and investigating their significance for axiomatization, interpretation, and the implementation of various information-theoretic tasks.

Pedro Vieira.name: Pedro Vieira
Pedro Vieira.position: The Clay Riddell Paul Dirac Chair in Theoretical Physics
Pedro Vieira.summary: Pedro Vieira is a The Clay Riddell Paul Dirac Chair in Theoretical Physics, Research Faculty Perimeter Research Chair. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics and Quantum Fields and Strings. Pedro Vieira's current research aims at developing new techniques for studying quantum field theories and string theories at finite coupling.

Chong Wang.name: Chong Wang
Chong Wang.summary: Chong Wang is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Matter. Chong Wang is interested in exotic quantum phases of matter, especially those arising from strongly correlated many-body systems. In particular Chong Wang is interested in topological phases of matter, quantum spin liquids, quantum Hall effects, quantum phase transitions and related topics in quantum field theories such as anomalies and dualities.

Mayuko Yamashita.name: Mayuko Yamashita
Mayuko Yamashita.position: Hideki Yukawa Early Career Chair in Theoretical Physics
Mayuko Yamashita.summary: Mayuko Yamashita is a Hideki Yukawa Early Career Chair in Theoretical Physics, Research Faculty Perimeter Research Chair. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics.

Beni Yoshida.name: Beni Yoshida
Beni Yoshida.summary: Beni Yoshida is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Information and Quantum Matter. Beni Yoshida focuses on applications of quantum information theory to problems of quantum many-body physics and quantum gravity.

Sisi Zhou.name: Sisi Zhou
Sisi Zhou.summary: Sisi Zhou is a Research Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Information. Sisi Zhou's research interests include quantum metrology (estimation theory), quantum error correction and quantum learning.

Katherine Mack.name: Katherine Mack
Katherine Mack.position: Hawking Chair in Cosmology and Science Communication
Katherine Mack.summary: Katherine Mack is a Hawking Chair in Cosmology and Science Communication, Outreach Faculty. Their research is connected to Cosmology. Katherine Mack's primary research interests are in theoretical cosmology, astroparticle physics, and galaxy formation. Katherine Mack is particularly interested in the physics and astrophysics of dark matter, and how researchers might find clues about dark matter's fundamental nature by examining its impact on the formation and evolution of the earliest cosmic structures.

Ghazal Geshnizjani.name: Ghazal Geshnizjani
Ghazal Geshnizjani.role: Teaching Faculty Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs
Ghazal Geshnizjani.summary: Ghazal Geshnizjani is a Teaching Faculty, Teaching Faculty Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs. Their research is connected to Cosmology.

Lauren Hayward.name: Lauren Hayward
Lauren Hayward.role: Teaching Faculty Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs
Lauren Hayward.summary: Lauren Hayward is an Associate Director, Training Programs, Teaching Faculty Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs. Their research is connected to Quantum Matter. Lauren works within the field of computational quantum matter physics, and her research interests involve using numerical methods to study phases of quantum many-body systems.

Aldo Riello.name: Aldo Riello
Aldo Riello.role: Teaching Faculty Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs
Aldo Riello.summary: Aldo Riello is a Teaching Faculty, Teaching Faculty Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics and Quantum Gravity. At the PSI Master's Program 2023-24 Classical Physics (appointed lecturer) Quantum Gravity (appointed lecturer) Gravitational Physics (assistant) 2022-23 Quantum Gravity (appointed lecturer) Gravitational Physics (assistant) Relativity (assistant) 2021-22 Quantum Gravity (appointed lecturer) 2020 Quantum Gravity (assistant) Aldo Riello has also thought courses at the University of Waterloo and at AIMS Senegal.

Neil Turok.name: Neil Turok
Neil Turok.position: Carlo Fidani Roger Penrose Distinguished Visiting Research Chair in Theoretical Physics
Neil Turok.summary: Neil Turok is a Carlo Fidani Roger Penrose Distinguished Visiting Research Chair in Theoretical Physics, Perimeter Research Chair. Their research is connected to Cosmology. Neil Turok (PhD Imperial College London, 1983) holds the Carlo Fidani Roger Penrose Distinguished Visiting Research Chair in Theoretical Physics. Turok's research focuses on developing fundamental theories of cosmology and new observational tests.

Savas Dimopoulos.name: Savas Dimopoulos
Savas Dimopoulos.position: Coril Holdings Archimedes Chair in Theoretical Physics (Visiting)
Savas Dimopoulos.summary: Savas Dimopoulos is a Coril Holdings Archimedes Chair in Theoretical Physics (Visiting), Perimeter Research Chair (Visiting). Their research is connected to Particle Physics.

Avery Broderick.name: Avery Broderick
Avery Broderick.summary: Avery Broderick is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Cosmology and Strong Gravity. Avery Broderick's research program aims to elucidate the fundamental nature and astronomical consequences of astrophysical black holes. Avery Broderick's current focus is the interpretation and analysis of horizon-resolving millimeter wave observations of the supermassive black holes at the centers of the Milky Way and M87.

Niayesh Afshordi.name: Niayesh Afshordi
Niayesh Afshordi.summary: Niayesh Afshordi is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Cosmology, Quantum Gravity, and Strong Gravity. Niayesh Afshordi is an Astronomer, and a Physicist; in short Niayesh Afshordi is an astrophysicist. Niayesh Afshordi is an Assistant Professor in the Astrophysics and Gravitation group at the Department of Physics and Astronomy University of Waterloo.

Alexander Braverman.name: Alexander Braverman
Alexander Braverman.summary: Alexander Braverman is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics. Alexander Braverman's main area of research is geometric representation theory (with applications to algebraic geometry, number theory and mathematical physics).

Cliff Burgess.name: Cliff Burgess
Cliff Burgess.summary: Cliff Burgess is Research Associate Faculty whose work is connected to cosmology and particle physics. His profile is strongest for matches involving theoretical physics, cosmology, and fundamental particle research.

Anton Burkov.name: Anton Burkov
Anton Burkov.summary: Anton Burkov is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Matter. Anton Burkov is interested in topological phases of matter and the interplay of nontrivial electronic structure topology and electron-electron interactions.

David Gosset.name: David Gosset
David Gosset.summary: David Gosset is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Information. David Gosset is interested in quantum algorithms, classical simulation of quantum computers, and the complexity of quantum many-body systems.

Matthew Johnson.name: Matthew Johnson
Matthew Johnson.summary: Matthew Johnson is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Cosmology. Matthew Johnson's research aims to understand the fundamental laws of nature through their impact on cosmology. Here is a sampling of questions that Matthew Johnson's research aims to answer: How big is the universe?

Theo Johnson-Freyd.name: Theo Johnson-Freyd
Theo Johnson-Freyd.summary: Theo Johnson-Freyd is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics. Theo Johnson-Freyd studies applications of higher category theory and homotopy theory to quantum field theory and to condensed matter, and Theo Johnson-Freyd studies applications of condensed matter and quantum field theory to homotopy theory and higher category theory. Theo Johnson-Freyd's current focus is on "global categorical symmetry": the study of topological extended objects in quantum field theories.

Raymond Laflamme.name: Raymond Laflamme
Raymond Laflamme.summary: Raymond Laflamme is a 1960 - 2025, Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Information.

Sung-Sik Lee.name: Sung-Sik Lee
Sung-Sik Lee.summary: Sung-Sik Lee is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Fields and Strings and Quantum Matter. The main goal of Sung-Sik Lee's research is to identify new dynamical principles that are behind novel emergent phenomena in critical states of quantum matter such as non-Fermi liquids. Sung-Sik Lee is also interested in the interdisciplinary research areas between condensed matter physics, string theory, and gravitational theory.

Debbie Leung.name: Debbie Leung
Debbie Leung.summary: Debbie Leung is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Information. Debbie Leung studies the capacity of communicating various form of data (quantum, private, or classical) through quantum channels, focusing on the superadditivity phenomenon. Tolman Postdoctoral Fellow, Theoretical Physics Institute for Quantum Information, Caltech, 2002-2005 Postdoctoral Fellow, Quantum Computation Mathematical Sciences Research Institute, Berkeley, 2002 Postdoctoral Fellow, Information group IBM T.J.

Roger Melko.name: Roger Melko
Roger Melko.summary: Roger Melko is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Matter. Roger Melko's group studies strongly-correlated quantum many-body systems, with a focus on emergent phenomena, novel phases and phase transitions, quantum criticality, and entanglement. With these techniques, Roger Melko's group explores low-energy physics in quantum magnets, cold atoms in optical lattices, bosonic fluids, and quantum computers.

Michele Mosca.name: Michele Mosca
Michele Mosca.summary: Michele Mosca is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Information. Canada Research Chair in Quantum Computation Co-founder of the Institute for Quantum Computing Founding member of the Perimeter Institute.

Christine Muschik.name: Christine Muschik
Christine Muschik.summary: Christine Muschik is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Quantum Information. Christine Muschik holds a University Research Chair at the University of Waterloo. Christine Muschik received a number of awards for her work on quantum computers, including an Ontario Early Researcher Award, a CIFAR Azrieli Global Scholar Fellowship for "Research Leaders of Tomorrow", and a Sloan Fellowship for outstanding early career researchers.

Ue-Li Pen.name: Ue-Li Pen
Ue-Li Pen.summary: Ue-Li Pen is Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Cosmology. Pen works in astrophysics and cosmology, especially in settings where large-scale collaborations and innovative observational initiatives can open new scientific opportunities.

Will Percival.name: Will Percival
Will Percival.summary: Will Percival is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Cosmology. Will Percival works in the field of cosmology, using observations to test theoretical models. Will Percival focuses particularly on galaxy surveys, using the positions of galaxies to measure the cosmological expansion rate and growth of cosmological structure.

Sergey Sibiryakov.name: Sergey Sibiryakov
Sergey Sibiryakov.summary: Sergey Sibiryakov is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Cosmology and Particle Physics. Sergey Sibiryakov's research lies in the interface of quantum field theory, particle physics, gravitation and cosmology. Sergey Sibiryakov is interested in understanding the fundamental principles underlying the laws of Nature, the interplay between them and the ways to test them.

Ben Webster.name: Ben Webster
Ben Webster.summary: Ben Webster is a Research Associate Faculty. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics. Ben Webster's research is on connections between representation theory, mathematical physics, geometry and topology, including knot homology, the geometry of symplectic singularities, and categorification. Before Ben Webster moved to Canada, Ben Webster's research was supported by the NSF under a CAREER award , and graduate and postdoctoral fellowships, and by a Sloan Research Fellowship.

Nathan Haouzi.name: Nathan Haouzi
Nathan Haouzi.summary: Nathan Haouzi is a Teaching Fellow, Academic Staff. Their research is connected to Quantum Fields and Strings.

Naren Manjunath.name: Naren Manjunath
Naren Manjunath.role: Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs
Naren Manjunath.summary: Naren Manjunath is a PSI Fellow, Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs. Their research is connected to Quantum Matter.

David Schmid.name: David Schmid
David Schmid.role: Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs
David Schmid.summary: David Schmid is a PSI Fellow, Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs. Their research is connected to Quantum Foundations. Understanding the interplay of entanglement and nonlocality: motivating and developing a new branch of entanglement theory.

Dan Wohns.name: Dan Wohns
Dan Wohns.role: Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs
Dan Wohns.summary: Dan Wohns is a Lecturer in academic training, educational outreach, and scientific programs. His profile is connected to cosmology and physics education.

Gang Xu.name: Gang Xu
Gang Xu.role: Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs
Gang Xu.summary: Gang Xu is a PSI Lecturer, Academic Staff Training, Educational Outreach and Scientific Programs. Their research is connected to Cosmology.

Estelle Maeva Inack.name: Estelle Maeva Inack
Estelle Maeva Inack.summary: Estelle Maeva Inack is a Research Scientist. Their research is connected to Quantum Matter. Machine learning, quantum computing, quantum Monte Carlo.

Geoffrey Ryan.name: Geoffrey Ryan
Geoffrey Ryan.summary: Geoffrey Ryan is a Research Scientist. Their research is connected to Strong Gravity. Eccentric Binaries Accreting from Thin Disks: Orbital Evolution.

Elie Wolfe.name: Elie Wolfe
Elie Wolfe.summary: Elie Wolfe is a Research Scientist. Their research is connected to Quantum Foundations and Quantum Information. Elie Wolfe studies how quantum resources can be used to generate non-classical correlations, ultimately seeking a quantitative characterization and a qualitative explanation of quantum correlations. In particular Elie Wolfe is interested in: • Causal structure in the language of Bayesian Directed Acyclic Graphs • Quantifying the quantum-over-classical advantage in network-based information-theoretic tasks • Contrasting quantum Hilbert space dimension with classical.

Achim Kempf.name: Achim Kempf
Achim Kempf.summary: Achim Kempf is a Research Associate. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics. He was a postdoc and a College Research Fellow (Corpus) at the Department of Applied Mathematics and Theoretical Physics (DAMTP) at Cambridge and a postdoc at the Institute for Fundamental Theory at University of Florida. He has been Canada Research Chair, for the Physics of Information for 10 years, he holds a University Research Chair and he is currently co-chair.

Steve MacLean.name: Steve MacLean
Steve MacLean.summary: Steve MacLean is a Senior Research Affiliate, Research Associate. Detailed public research notes are limited in the current source material, but this profile is included with the scientific research group.

John Moffat.name: John Moffat
John Moffat.summary: John Moffat is a Senior Research Affiliate, Research Associate. Their research is connected to Cosmology, Particle Physics, Quantum Gravity, and Strong Gravity. John went on to a Senior Scientist position at the Research Institute for Advanced Studies in Maryland. The following topics of research are being pursued: 1) Investigations of the standard model of particle physics 2) Cosmology 3) Modified gravitational theory 4) Quantum gravity 5) Astrophysics and astronomy (analyzes of observational data).

Rafael Sorkin.name: Rafael Sorkin
Rafael Sorkin.summary: Rafael Sorkin is a Researcher Emeritus, Research Associate. Their research is connected to Cosmology, Quantum Foundations, and Quantum Gravity. Along with topology and topology-change, black hole entropy has long interested Rafael as another clue to the nature of quantum gravity. The lack of a theory of quantum gravity is an obvious symptom of the disunity in our present understanding, but it is not the only symptom one can point to.

Federico Ambrosino.name: Federico Ambrosino
Federico Ambrosino.summary: Federico Ambrosino is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Quantum Fields and Strings.

Rodrigo Andrade E Silva.name: Rodrigo Andrade E Silva
Rodrigo Andrade E Silva.summary: Rodrigo Andrade E Silva is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Quantum Gravity.

Roberto Baldijao.name: Roberto Baldijao
Roberto Baldijao.summary: Roberto Baldijao is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Detailed public research notes are limited in the current source material, but this profile is included with the scientific research group.

Caio B. de S. Nascimento.name: Caio B. de S. Nascimento
Caio B. de S. Nascimento.summary: Caio B. de S. Nascimento is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Cosmology.

Roland Bittleston.name: Roland Bittleston
Roland Bittleston.summary: Roland Bittleston is a Senior Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics. Roland Bittleston's research centres on the intersection of integrability, twistor theory and quantum field theory. Together with collaborators, Roland Bittleston is investigating how twistor theory can be used to understand the integrability of 4d quantum field theories, particularly self-dual gravity.

Sihao Cheng.name: Sihao Cheng
Sihao Cheng.summary: Sihao Cheng is a Senior Postdoctoral Research Fellow, Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Cosmology.

Changha Choi.name: Changha Choi
Changha Choi.summary: Changha Choi is a Richard Feynman Fellow, Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Quantum Fields and Strings.

Luca Ciambelli.name: Luca Ciambelli
Luca Ciambelli.summary: Luca Ciambelli is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Quantum Fields and Strings and Quantum Gravity. Classical and Quantum Gravity, 41(16), 165011.

Luciano Combi.name: Luciano Combi
Luciano Combi.position: Joint Postdoctoral Research Fellow - University of Guelph/Perimeter
Luciano Combi.summary: Luciano Combi is a Joint Postdoctoral Research Fellow with the University of Guelph and Perimeter Institute. His research is connected to strong gravity and relativistic astrophysics.

Marco Costa.name: Marco Costa
Marco Costa.summary: Marco Costa is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Particle Physics.

Marina De Amicis.name: Marina De Amicis
Marina De Amicis.summary: Marina De Amicis is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Strong Gravity.

Ana Ennis.name: Ana Ennis
Ana Ennis.position: Joint Postdoctoral Research Fellow - Perimeter/UW(Centre for Astrophysics)
Ana Ennis.summary: Ana Ennis is a Joint Postdoctoral Research Fellow - Perimeter/UW(Centre for Astrophysics), Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Cosmology. Ana Ennis is a postdoctoral fellow at Perimeter Institute and University of Waterloo, working on the study of extragalactic globular cluster systems. Ana Ennis's main interest is galaxy formation and evolution seen through the study of discrete tracers.

Encieh Erfani.name: Encieh Erfani
Encieh Erfani.summary: Encieh Erfani is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Detailed public research notes are limited in the current source material, but this profile is included with the scientific research group.

Tamara Evstafyeva.name: Tamara Evstafyeva
Tamara Evstafyeva.summary: Tamara Evstafyeva is a Vera Florence Cooper Rubin Fellow, Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Strong Gravity.

Michael Fedderke.name: Michael Fedderke
Michael Fedderke.summary: Michael Fedderke is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Particle Physics.

Raz Firanko.name: Raz Firanko
Raz Firanko.summary: Raz Firanko is a Joint Postdoctoral Research Fellow - Perimeter/IQC, Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Quantum Information and Quantum Matter.

Suroor Seher Gandhi.name: Suroor Seher Gandhi
Suroor Seher Gandhi.summary: Suroor Seher Gandhi is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Cosmology.

Pranay Gorantla.name: Pranay Gorantla
Pranay Gorantla.summary: Pranay Gorantla is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Detailed public research notes are limited in the current source material, but this profile is included with the scientific research group.

James Gurian.name: James Gurian
James Gurian.summary: James Gurian is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Cosmology.

Yaroslav Herasymenko.name: Yaroslav Herasymenko
Yaroslav Herasymenko.summary: Yaroslav Herasymenko is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics, Quantum Information, and Quantum Matter.

Selim Hotinli.name: Selim Hotinli
Selim Hotinli.summary: Selim Hotinli is a P. J. E. Peebles Fellow, Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Cosmology.

Christopher Jackson.name: Christopher Jackson
Christopher Jackson.summary: Christopher Jackson is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Quantum Foundations and Quantum Information.

Joshua Kirklin.name: Joshua Kirklin
Joshua Kirklin.summary: Joshua Kirklin is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Quantum Gravity.

Simon Langenscheidt.name: Simon Langenscheidt
Simon Langenscheidt.summary: Simon Langenscheidt is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Quantum Gravity.

Ryan Lanzetta.name: Ryan Lanzetta
Ryan Lanzetta.summary: Ryan Lanzetta is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Quantum Matter.

Sizheng Ma.name: Sizheng Ma
Sizheng Ma.summary: Sizheng Ma is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Strong Gravity.

Mariem Magdy.name: Mariem Magdy
Mariem Magdy.summary: Mariem Magdy is a Joint Postdoctoral Research Fellow - Perimeter/Fields/Aims, Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics.

Anindita Maiti.name: Anindita Maiti
Anindita Maiti.summary: Anindita Maiti is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Mathematical Physics, Quantum Fields and Strings, and Quantum Matter.

Yusuke Manita.name: Yusuke Manita
Yusuke Manita.summary: Yusuke Manita is a Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Cosmology.

Alexander Meiburg.name: Alexander Meiburg
Alexander Meiburg.summary: Alexander Meiburg is a Joint Postdoctoral Research Fellow IQC/Perimeter, Postdoctoral Researcher. Their research is connected to Quantum Information.

Peter Abbamonte.name: Peter Abbamonte
research_areas: electron self-organization in condensed matter, stripe phases, topological order
edge and interface effects in oxide devices
collective excitations in interacting electron systems
Peter Abbamonte.summary: Peter Abbamonte is a Fox Family Professor in Engineering in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to quantum metrology and sensing, electron self-organization in condensed matter, stripe phases, topological order, edge and interface effects in oxide devices, quantum phase transitions, and collective excitations in interacting electron systems. Professor Abbamonte received his Ph.D from the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign in 1999, having done his research with the then thriving Materials Physics Department at Bell Laboratories. He then went to the University of Groningen in The Netherlands on an IRFAP fellowship from the National Science Foundation.

Chris Anderson.name: Chris Anderson
Chris Anderson.summary: Chris Anderson is an Assistant Professor in the Materials Science and Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking, Quantum Sensing, and Quantum Materials. The Anderson lab combines materials science, applied physics, and electrical engineering to manipulate and generate photons with solid-state materials. Chris Anderson's group is interested in enabling these applications by using wafer-scalable semiconductor spin qubits to form quantum repeaters with photonic devices, and by harnessing materials near quantum phase transitions to make giant optical nonlinearities.

Mikael Backlund.name: Mikael Backlund
Mikael Backlund.summary: Mikael Backlund is an Assistant Professor in the Chemistry Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Sensing. Research interests in the Backlund Group are centered on applied quantum sensing and microscopy methods for the investigation of molecular-scale systems. One thrust of the lab involves using quantum defects in diamond to parse complex phenomena in targets relevant to biophysics and soft condensed matter.

Gaurav Bahl.name: Gaurav Bahl
Gaurav Bahl.summary: Gaurav Bahl is a George B. Grim Professor in the Mechanical Science and Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking. Research emphasis is on ultrahigh-Q optomechanical systems and on mechanisms by which chiral transport of photons and phonons can be produced.

Douglas Beck.name: Douglas Beck
Douglas Beck.summary: Douglas Beck is a Professor and Director of NPL in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Physics, Electron Scattering, Momentum Transfer, and Deuterons.

Alexey Bezryadin.name: Alexey Bezryadin
Alexey Bezryadin.summary: Alexey Bezryadin is a Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Materials. Alexey Berzryadin and his group conduct research on novel superconducting qubits, such as topologically protected Majorana qubits, nanowire-based qubits, and molecular qubits. include transduction and storage of quantum information as well as short and long-range coupling between qubits based on different physical mechanisms.

Simeon Bogdanov.name: Simeon Bogdanov
Simeon Bogdanov.position: Assistant Professor Science Advisory Board Member
Simeon Bogdanov.summary: Simeon Bogdanov is an Assistant Professor Science Advisory Board Member in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking, Quantum Sensing, and Quantum Materials.

Barry Bradlyn.name: Barry Bradlyn
Barry Bradlyn.summary: Barry Bradlyn is an Associate Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Materials. Barry Bradlyn is a theoretical condensed matter physicist studying topological properties of quantum materials as a platform for sensing and computation, as well as using topology and geometry to find new ways of controlling light in metamaterials.

David G. Cahill.name: David G. Cahill
David G. Cahill.position: Professor and Grainger Distinguished Chair in Engineering
David G. Cahill.summary: David G. Cahill is a Professor and Grainger Distinguished Chair in Engineering in the Materials Science and Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Sensing and Quantum Materials. He uses ultrafast experimental techniques to probe the fundamental time-scales of scattering processes that control coherence and the conduction of thermal excitations.

Roy Campbell.name: Roy Campbell
Roy Campbell.position: Sohaib and Sara Abbasi Professor Emeritus
Roy Campbell.summary: Roy Campbell is the Sohaib and Sara Abbasi Professor Emeritus in the Computer Science Department. His profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group and is relevant to computing, systems, and applied technology work.

David Ceperley.name: David Ceperley
David Ceperley.summary: David Ceperley is a Professor in the Physics Department. Their profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group. Springfield Avenue David Ceperley has a long history in high performance computing. In collaboration with DeMarco, he investigated cold atom emulator for many-body systems.

Eric Chitambar.name: Eric Chitambar
Eric Chitambar.position: Professor Executive Committee Member Science Advisory Board Member
Eric Chitambar.summary: Eric Chitambar is a Professor Executive Committee Member Science Advisory Board Member in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking and Quantum Information Theory. Eric Chitambar is a quantum information theorist working in the areas of quantum communication, entanglement theory, nonlocality, and general quantum resource theories. One primary interest involves understanding the interplay between quantum and classical information processing in multi-party scenarios.

Bryan Clark.name: Bryan Clark
Bryan Clark.position: Associate Professor Science Advisory Board Member
Bryan Clark.summary: Bryan Clark is an Associate Professor Science Advisory Board Member in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Computing and Quantum Information Theory. Bryan Clark's group is especially interested in algorithms for quantum simulation; shadow tomography approaches, and ways to mitigate errors. Bryan Clark's group also studies the dividing line between classical and quantum simulations looking at various classical algorithms for simulating subsets of quantum circuits.

Offir Cohen.name: Offir Cohen
Offir Cohen.summary: Offir Cohen is a Research Scientist in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking and Quantum Sensing. Particular interests include the generation of new photonic quantum states and the quantum nature of light-matter interactions. Atomic, molecular and optical physics, quantum information, nonlinear spectroscopy, and modeling of light-matter interaction.

Jacob Covey.name: Jacob Covey
Jacob Covey.summary: Jacob Covey is an Assistant Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Research. Covey ’s research utilizes arrays of individually controlled neutral alkaline-earth atoms in optical tweezers to engineer many-body entangled states. Applications of interest include distributed quantum computing, quantum communication, and quantum-enhanced metrology with atomic array optical clocks.

Brian DeMarco.name: Brian DeMarco
Brian DeMarco.summary: Brian DeMarco is a Director, IQUIST in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Computing and Quantum Networking. He is launching new QIS efforts, including a joint project to realize cluster-state quantum computing using ultracold molecules and the IQUIST distributed ion-trap quantum computing testbed.

Patrick Draper.name: Patrick Draper
Patrick Draper.summary: Patrick Draper is an Associate Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Chromodynamics, Supersymmetry, and Higgs bosons. Much of Patrick Draper's research has been motivated by the electroweak hierarchy problem and the strong CP problem. Patrick Draper has worked on the physics of the Higgs boson, particularly in the context of supersymmetric solutions to the hierarchy problem; the cosmology of axion solutions to the strong CP problem and other axion-like dark matter candidates; and the experimental probes of other mechanisms that could address strong CP.

Jim Eckstein.name: Jim Eckstein
Jim Eckstein.summary: Jim Eckstein is a Professor in the Physics Department. Their profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group.

Emily Edwards.name: Emily Edwards
Emily Edwards.summary: Emily Edwards is an Adjunct Research Assistant Professor in the Materials Research Laboratory Department. Their profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group.

Aida El-Khadra.name: Aida El-Khadra
Aida El-Khadra.summary: Aida El-Khadra is a Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Applied Quantum Information Science.

Kejie Fang.name: Kejie Fang
Kejie Fang.summary: Kejie Fang is an Associate Professor in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking.

Majed Fataftah.name: Majed Fataftah
Majed Fataftah.position: Assistant Professor of Chemistry CLSL A428
Majed Fataftah.summary: Majed Fataftah is an Assistant Professor of Chemistry CLSL A428 in the Chemistry Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Sensing and Quantum Materials. Majed received a B.S. in chemistry and biochemistry from the University of Washington in 2013 where he conducted research in the lab of Professor Daniel R. He received his Ph.D. from Northwestern University in 2019 under the tutelage of Professor Danna E.

Tom Faulkner.name: Tom Faulkner
Tom Faulkner.summary: Tom Faulkner is an Associate Professor in the Physics Department. Their profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group. They have uncovered fundamental connections between bounds on the processing of quantum information and the dynamics of quantum gravity and QFT.

Eduardo Fradkin.name: Eduardo Fradkin
Eduardo Fradkin.position: Donald Biggar Willett Professor in Engineering and Director of ICMT
Eduardo Fradkin.summary: Eduardo Fradkin is a Donald Biggar Willett Professor in Engineering and Director of ICMT in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Materials.

Bryce Gadway.name: Bryce Gadway
Bryce Gadway.summary: Bryce Gadway is an Adjunct Professor in the Physics Department. Their profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group.

Matthew Gilbert.name: Matthew Gilbert
Matthew Gilbert.summary: Matthew Gilbert is an Associate Professor in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Materials. Matthew Gilbert is broadly working on the theory of topology and topological quantum information processing. The end goal of the work is to elucidate the role, if any, of topology and topological materials in the future of information processing architectures.

Elizabeth Goldschmidt.name: Elizabeth Goldschmidt
Elizabeth Goldschmidt.summary: Elizabeth Goldschmidt is an Associate Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking, Quantum Sensing, and Quantum Materials.

Axel Hoffmann.name: Axel Hoffmann
Axel Hoffmann.summary: Axel Hoffmann is a Founder Professor in the Materials Science and Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking and Quantum Materials. In particular he focuses on tailoring interactions with other quantum states, e.g., photons and phonons, and the engineering of magnon propagation.

Taylor Hughes.name: Taylor Hughes
Taylor Hughes.summary: Taylor Hughes is a Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Materials.

Marius Junge.name: Marius Junge
Marius Junge.summary: Marius Junge is a Professor in the Math Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Information Theory.

Yonatan Kahn.name: Yonatan Kahn
Yonatan Kahn.summary: Yonatan Kahn is an Assistant Professor in the Physics Department. Their profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group. The detection mechanisms rely heavily on quantum sensing and quantum materials.

Dakshita Khurana.name: Dakshita Khurana
Dakshita Khurana.summary: Dakshita Khurana is an Associate Professor in the Computer Science Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Information Theory. Khurana's research has focused on designing protocols for secure quantum multi-party computation. Khurana's research so far in this area has led to the three publications listed above, where the first one demonstrates the feasibility of multi-party classical computation with security against quantum adversaries, and the second one aims to understand the amount of back-and-forth interaction required for secure quantum computation.

Kohei Kishida.name: Kohei Kishida
Kohei Kishida.summary: Kohei Kishida is an Assistant Professor in the Philosophy Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Information Theory. Wright Street Kohei Kishida is a logician-philosopher working on foundations of quantum physics, information, and computing. His research in QIS centers on quantum non-locality and contextuality as computational resources.

Angela Kou.name: Angela Kou
Angela Kou.summary: Angela Kou is an Assistant Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Computing, Quantum Networking, and Quantum Materials.

Paul Kwiat.name: Paul Kwiat
Paul Kwiat.position: Professor Executive Committee Member Science Advisory Board Member
Paul Kwiat.summary: Paul Kwiat is a Professor Executive Committee Member Science Advisory Board Member in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking and Quantum Sensing.

Felix Leditzky.name: Felix Leditzky
Felix Leditzky.summary: Felix Leditzky is an Assistant Professor in the Math Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Information Theory.

Jong Yeon Lee.name: Jong Yeon Lee
Jong Yeon Lee.summary: Jong Yeon Lee is an Assistant Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Computing and Quantum Information Theory. In the groups of John Preskill and Olexei Motrunich, he worked on quantum state tomography algorithms and exotic quantum criticalities. After, he went on to receive his Ph.D. from Harvard University in 2020, under the supervision of Ashvin Vishwanath.

Minjoo Lawrence Lee.name: Minjoo Lawrence Lee
Minjoo Lawrence Lee.summary: Minjoo Lawrence Lee is a Professor Executive Committee Member in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking.

Tony Leggett.name: Tony Leggett
Tony Leggett.summary: Tony Leggett is a Professor Emeritus in the Physics Department. Their profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group.

Rob Leigh.name: Rob Leigh
Rob Leigh.summary: Rob Leigh is a Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Information Theory.

Virginia Lorenz.name: Virginia Lorenz
Virginia Lorenz.position: Associate Professor Science Advisory Board Member
Virginia Lorenz.summary: Virginia Lorenz is an Associate Professor Science Advisory Board Member in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Networking and Quantum Sensing.

Vidya Madhavan.name: Vidya Madhavan
Vidya Madhavan.position: Professor Executive Committee Member Science Advisory Board Member
Vidya Madhavan.summary: Vidya Madhavan is a Professor Executive Committee Member Science Advisory Board Member in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Materials.

Fahad Mahmood.name: Fahad Mahmood
Fahad Mahmood.summary: Fahad Mahmood is an Associate Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Materials. Fahad Mahmood’s research utilizes coherent light-matter interaction at ultrafast (fs to ps) timescales to engineer electronic and magnetic interactions and symmetries in quantum materials, probe their spatial and temporal correlations and realize novel non-equilibrium phases of matter.

Nancy Makri.name: Nancy Makri
research_areas: Theoretical quantum dynamics of large molecules and condensed phase systems
Nancy Makri.summary: Nancy Makri is a Professor in the Chemistry Department. Their work is connected to Theoretical quantum dynamics of large molecules and condensed phase systems, Charge transfer reactions, and Quantum control of nanostructures. Nancy Makri's group strives to advance theoretical understanding of quantum mechanical processes in large molecules and the condensed phase. Full solution of the Schrödinger equation is feasible only for small molecules, as the required numerical effort increases exponentially with the number of particles.

Nadya Mason.name: Nadya Mason
Nadya Mason.summary: Nadya Mason is an Adjunct Professor in the Physics Department. Their profile is part of the University of Illinois quantum engineering group. Nadya Mason’s research measures electrical transport properties of quantum materials, e.g., superconducting, topological and 2D. The work is relevant to quantum coherence, quantum metrology, and science and materials for qubits.

Wolfgang Pfaff.name: Wolfgang Pfaff
Wolfgang Pfaff.summary: Wolfgang Pfaff is an Assistant Professor in the Physics Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Computing and Quantum Networking. Wolfgang Pfaff's group is interested in how quantum states can be protected and distributed in open systems. Wolfgang Pfaff's group explores how couplings between quantum objects (such as qubits) as well as couplings to the environment can be controlled to stabilize quantum states or render them insensitive to decoherence.

Andre Schleife.name: Andre Schleife
research_areas: Materials for energy and the environment
Andre Schleife.summary: Andre Schleife is a Professor in the Materials Science and Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Computational Material Science, Electronic Materials, and Materials for energy and the environment. Schleife develops computational approaches for understanding excited electronic states and their dynamics in materials.

Thomas Searles.name: Thomas Searles
Thomas Searles.summary: Thomas Searles is an Associate Professor in the Electrical and Computer Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum computing, Quantum information, Quantum materials, and Quantum networking. Searles currently serves as an Associate Professor in the Electrical & Computer Engineering at the University of Illinois Chicago, hired under the University of Illinois System Distinguished Faculty Recruitment Program. Thomas received his Ph.D. in Applied Physics from Rice University in 2011, where his thesis work primarily focused on the magneto-optical properties of carbon nanotubes.

Daniel Shoemaker.name: Daniel Shoemaker
Daniel Shoemaker.summary: Daniel Shoemaker is an Associate Professor in the Materials Science and Engineering Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Materials. The Shoemaker group synthesizes new and underexplored materials with correlated electrons and explores chemical methods of tuning their behavior. Crystal growth, optical and structural characterization, magnetism, X-ray and neutron scattering, and computational explorations of phase space are all relevant for emerging materials in quantum information science.

Makrand Sinha.name: Makrand Sinha
Makrand Sinha.summary: Makrand Sinha is an Assistant Professor in the Computer Science Department. Their work is connected to Quantum Computing and Quantum Information Theory. Professor Makrand's broad research goal is to understand the scope of applications of quantum algorithms, both in the near-term and long-term. He is also quite interested in the connections between quantum computing and other areas of physics and mathematics.

Arman Zaribafiyan.name: Arman Zaribafiyan
Arman Zaribafiyan.summary: Arman Zaribafiyan is the CTO at 1QBit. Head of Quantum Computing at 1QBit, Arman Zaribafiyan completed his MSc in electrical and computer engineering with a focus on quantum information processing at the University of British Columbia. Passionate about emerging computing technologies, he is interested in connecting these to computationally challenging and intractable industry problems.

Scott Dufferwiel.name: Scott Dufferwiel
Scott Dufferwiel.summary: Scott Dufferwiel is the CTO at AEGIQ. Scott Dufferwiel is an entrepreneur at heart with a keen interest in emerging technologies with disruptive potential. As well as being AEGIQ’s CTO, Dufferwiel is also a co-founder of the Sheffield-based QC startup.

Matteo Rossi.name: Matteo Rossi
Matteo Rossi.summary: Matteo Rossi is the CTO at Algorithmiq. A postdoctoral researcher at the Quantum Technologies Group of the Turku Centre for Quantum Physics, University of Turku, Algorithmiq CTO and co-founder Matteo Rossi’s research in quantum information and quantum computing is helping his startup develop its novel IP in advanced algorithms to solve complex problems in life sciences through quantum information, complex systems and computational physics.

Prineha Narang.name: Prineha Narang
Prineha Narang.summary: Prineha Narang is the CTO at Aliro. As well as being Boston-based quantum startup Aliro’s co-founder and CTO, Prineha Narang is an assistant professor at the John A. Paulson School of Engineering and Applied Sciences at Harvard University.

David Bestwick.name: David Bestwick
David Bestwick.summary: David Bestwick is the CTO at Arqit. While being the CTO of cloud-based quantum encryption startup Arqit, David Bestwick is also a co-founder of the company, too. Before co-founding Arqit, Bestwick co-founded Avanti Communications, a startup that pioneered the use of Ka band satcoms to deploy a fleet of four high throughput geostationary telecom satellites serving EMEA.

Christian Nietner.name: Christian Nietner
Christian Nietner.summary: Christian Nietner is the CTO at Avanetix. Christian Nietner is the CTO and co-founder of the Berlin-based startup Avanetix, which — by applying classical optimization methods, ML and QC — provides a unique, vertically integrated and industry-specific application software stack that solves today’s and tomorrow’s supply chain challenges. Before all that, Nietner spent some years as a data scientist.

Rut Lineswala.name: Rut Lineswala
Rut Lineswala.summary: Rut Lineswala is the CTO at BosonQ Psi. CTO of Indian-based QC and multiphysics simulations startup BosonQ Psi, Rut Lineswala is currently getting his Ph.D. in the field of computational fluid dynamics (CFD) of hypersonic applications at the University of Minnesota. At BosonQ Psi, Lineswala leads the technical team and focuses on emerging technologies and different areas to expand into.

Yehuda Naveh.name: Yehuda Naveh
Yehuda Naveh.summary: Yehuda Naveh is the CTO at Classiq. Yehuda Naveh is CTO and co-founder of Israeli-based quantum startup Classiq, a company that provides a revolutionary quantum algorithm design software platform for the automated synthesis of quantum algorithms. A quantum physicist and computer scientist by training, before founding Classiq, Naveh was a research staff member at IBM for two decades.

Dana Anderson.name: Dana Anderson
Dana Anderson.summary: Dana Anderson is the CTO at ColdQuanta. CTO of Colorado-based QC company ColdQaunta, whose technology in laser-cooled atoms (including ions) is applied to a spectrum of quantum applications including timekeeping, inertial sensing, quantum communications, quantum networks and quantum computing, Dana Anderson is an applied physicist trained in quantum optics. With extensive experience in precision measurement, quantum technology, atomtronics, optical neural networks, signal processing, and quantum systems based on cold and ultra-cold atoms, at ColdQuanta he is responsible for maintaining the strategic and technical vision of the company.

Oliver Maynard.name: Oliver Maynard
Oliver Maynard.summary: Oliver Maynard is the CTO at Crypta Labs. Oliver Maynard is in charge of the technical development of Crypta Labs’ HSM — Cicada, with a particular focus on the commercialization of the product. Working in the IT sector since the mid-1990s, in 2016, Maynard sold a company he had founded and joined quantum-based encryption solution startup Crypta Labs as CTO.

Jean-Charles Faugère.name: Jean-Charles Faugère
Jean-Charles Faugère.summary: Jean-Charles Faugère is the CTO at CryptoNext Security. Jean-Charles Faugère is the co-founder and CTO of CryptoNext, a spinoff from Inria Paris and Sorbonne University. A pioneer in the area of quantum-safe cryptography, Faugère has impressively developed efficient algorithms and high-performance software that permit to assess the security of a large variety of quantum-safe cryptosystems.

Stephen Lyon.name: Stephen Lyon
Stephen Lyon.summary: Stephen Lyon is the CTO at EeroQ. Bringing decades of experience in semiconductor physics and engineering to leading quantum computing using electrons on helium startup EeroQ, Princeton University professor Stephen Lyon brought a lot to the company when he joined in the spring of this year. “This technology holds promise for fast submicron quantum gates, comparable to silicon-based qubits, but with long-coherence mobile qubits, like trapped ions, and with device structures which have been fabricated using an unmodified CMOS foundry process.

Ewan Munro.name: Ewan Munro
Ewan Munro.summary: Ewan Munro is the CTO at Entropica Labs. Ewan Munro is a theoretical physicist by training with a strong interest in algorithms, benchmarking, and product building. Leading Singapore’s Centre for Quantum Technologies (NUS) spinoff Entropica Lab’s technical strategy, he assists with the company’s quantum optimization and ML, supporting enterprise customers to understand and integrate quantum computing.

Elena Blokhina.name: Elena Blokhina
Elena Blokhina.summary: Elena Blokhina is the CTO at Equal1.labs. CTO of Silicon Valley/Dublin-based silicon semiconductor QC startup Equal1.labs, Blokhina specializes in microelectronic systems, multi-physics systems, quantum electronics, semiconductor devices, microelectromechanical systems (MEMS), and computer-aided design (CAD). An associate professor at University College Dublin, Blokhina has a Ph.D. in physical and mathematical sciences from Saratov State University, Russia.

Norbert Lütkenhaus.name: Norbert Lütkenhaus
Norbert Lütkenhaus.summary: Norbert Lütkenhaus is the CTO at EvolutionQ. CTO and co-founder of EvolutionQ, a Canadian quantum-safe cybersecurity startup, Norbert Lütkenhaus is a global expert in fields that encompass quantum communication, quantum cryptography and quantum-safe cybersecurity.

Jungsang Kim.name: Jungsang Kim
Jungsang Kim.summary: Jungsang Kim is the CTO at IonQ. Not really needing an introduction, Jungsang Kim is the co-founder and CTO of IonQ, a quantum computing hardware and software company based in College Park, Maryland. Currently developing a general-purpose trapped ion quantum computer and software to generate, optimize, and execute quantum circuits, Kim is helping to build the world’s best quantum computer to solve the world’s most complex problems.

Kuan Yen Tan.name: Kuan Yen Tan
Kuan Yen Tan.summary: Kuan Yen Tan is the CTO at IQM Finland. CTO and co-founder of Finnish QC startup IQM Finland, Kuan Yen Tan is an Academy Research Fellow at Aalto University, Finland. Based in Espoo, he leads the charge in IQM delivering on-premises quantum computers for research laboratories and supercomputing centres and provides complete access to its hardware while for industrial customers providing quantum advantage through a unique application-specific co-design approach.

Mike Brown.name: Mike Brown
Mike Brown.summary: Mike Brown is the CTO at ISARA Corporation. Mike Brown is the CTO and co-founder of security solutions company ISARA Corporation, which specializes in creating class-defining quantum-safe cryptography for today’s computing ecosystems. Based in the quantum hub of Waterloo, Ontario, Brown is responsible for the technical vision and direction of the startup, with his teams particularly focused on research in quantum-safe algorithms as well as how to implement them in a secure and efficient manner.

Thomas Decker.name: Thomas Decker
Thomas Decker.summary: Thomas Decker is the CTO at JoS Quantum. Joining JoS Quantum back in 2019, prior to this CTO Thomas Decker worked as a software developer for a high-performance database company where he was responsible for the optimization algorithms. Decker has held an impressive number of postdoctoral research positions, including at the University of Washington in Seattle, at McGill University in Montreal and at the Centre for Quantum Technologies in Singapore.

Philip Sibson.name: Philip Sibson
Philip Sibson.summary: Philip Sibson is the CTO at KETS Quantum Security. CTO and co-founder of KETS Quantum Security, a Bristol, UK-based startup developing miniaturized photonic communications technology to offer the ultimate in digital security, Philip Sibson is an experienced engineer and researcher with a Ph.D. in photonic quantum technologies from Bristol University. Sibson’s knowledge is sure to help KETS with the continuing development of its technologies for quantum-secured communications, including QKD and QRNG.

Jan Spallek.name: Jan Spallek
Jan Spallek.summary: Jan Spallek is the CTO at Kiutra. Jan Spallek is CTO and co-founder of kiutra, a German QC startup that is providing next-generation, cryogen-free cooling solutions for basic research, material science, quantum technology, high-performance electronics, and detector applications. Spallek heads the hardware development team and oversees IP creation.

Samuel Mugel.name: Samuel Mugel
Samuel Mugel.summary: Samuel Mugel is the CTO at Multiverse Computing. CTO of software provider for companies from the financial industry wanting to gain an edge with quantum computing, Multiverse Computing, Samuel Mugel is an expert in QC and quantum machine learning (QML). With a Ph.D. in ultracold atoms jointly awarded by ICFO (Spain) and the University of Southampton, Mugel’s work as CTO at Multiverse Computing encompasses implementing algorithms on present-day quantum computers, co-authoring articles about quantum computing in finance and developing quantum computing use-cases in finance.

Bill Shipman.name: Bill Shipman
Bill Shipman.summary: Bill Shipman is the CTO at POLARISqb. Bill Shipman is CTO and co-founder of POLARISqb, a startup utilizing QC, AI, ML and personalized medicine to disrupt drug design. With a demonstrated history of working in diverse scientific fields as a systems architect, systems developer, research scientist, as well as a director of cloud computing at Cloud Pharmaceuticals, Shipman is skilled in Python, Java, SQL, and C#.

Tomas Babej.name: Tomas Babej
Tomas Babej.summary: Tomas Babej is the CTO at ProteinQure. Co-founder and CTO at Canadian computational platform for the design of protein therapeutics ProteinQure, Tomas Babej an entrepreneur with a strong engineering background, interested in ML, QC and their intersection, quantum ML. Babej has a MA degree in algorithm design, optimization and QC from the University of Brno, Czech Republic.

Robert Love.name: Robert Love
Robert Love.summary: Robert Love is the CTO at Q-CTRL.

Jules van Oven.name: Jules van Oven
Jules van Oven.summary: Jules van Oven is the CTO at QBLOX. Jules van Oven is co-founder and CTO of Dutch startup Qblox, which as a spinoff of QuTech, designs quantum control stacks that can be easily integrated into its customers’ quantum computers. Van Oven has an MSc in applied physics from the Delft University of Technology.

Jean-Philippe Bourgoin.name: Jean-Philippe Bourgoin
Jean-Philippe Bourgoin.summary: Jean-Philippe Bourgoin is the CTO at QEYnet. Jean-Philippe Bourgoin is the CTO and co-founder of Toronto-based quantum startup QEYnet, developing a global, low-cost, microsatellite-enabled quantum key distribution network. Bourgoin has led development, testing, and field demonstrations of satellite payload component prototypes and supporting ground systems.

Robert Stockill.name: Robert Stockill
Robert Stockill.summary: Robert Stockill is the CTO at Qphox. Robert Stockill is the CTO and co-founder of yet another Netherlands-based startup, Qphox, out to building the world’s first quantum modem device, connecting quantum computers across a quantum network. With Qphox, Stockill is focused on trying to understand how researchers can construct large-scale quantum states, both by understanding in detail the dynamics of optically active quantum systems and by demonstrating the fundamental protocols for networked quantum information.

Denis Mandich.name: Denis Mandich
Denis Mandich.summary: Denis Mandich is the CTO at Qrypt. Denis Mandich is the CTO and co-founder of quantum secure encryption startup Qrypt. A physicist by training who holds multiple patents in cryptographic algorithms, cloud services and quantum hardware entropy sources, he is helping to build Qrypt’s post-quantum encryption systems to secure data in the quantum age.

Vincent Elfving.name: Vincent Elfving
Vincent Elfving.summary: Vincent Elfving is the CTO at Qu & Co. A quantum physicist specializing in quantum chemistry, quantum computing, machine learning, computational science and software development, Vincent Elfving is the CTO and co-founder of Amsterdam-based startup Qu & Co. The startup, founded in 2016, is developing quantum-computing algorithms, software and services running on currently available quantum hardware in areas such as computational chemistry, computational finance, machine learning and combinatorial optimization, and Elfving is very much a part of this central effort.

Niccolo Somaschi.name: Niccolo Somaschi
Niccolo Somaschi.summary: Niccolo Somaschi is the CTO at Quandela. Niccolo Somaschi is the CTO and co-founder of French startup Quandela. Somaschi’s role as CTO is focused on the development of quantum technologies for real-life applications.

Nitzan Livneh.name: Nitzan Livneh
Nitzan Livneh.summary: Nitzan Livneh is the CTO at Quant LR. CTO at Israeli quantum cryptography startup Quant LR, Nitzan Livneh specializes in optics and quantum communication. Livneh has a Ph.D. in applied physics from the Hebrew University of Jerusalem.

Michael Redding.name: Michael Redding
Michael Redding.summary: Michael Redding is the CTO at Quantropi. Michael Redding has been the CTO of Canadian cyberSecurity company Quantropi since March of this year. Before Quantropi, Redding was the managing director and co-founder of Accenture Ventures, where he grew a global portfolio of strategic partnerships and 38 equity investments in emerging technology startups.

Yonatan Cohen.name: Yonatan Cohen
Yonatan Cohen.summary: Yonatan Cohen is the CTO at Quantum Machines. Entrepreneur and ex-managing director of the Weizmann Institute’s entrepreneurship program, Yonatan Cohen is the co-founder and CTO of Israeli-based Quantum Machines, a startup developing innovative software, hardware and electronics in QC.

Jon Ander Oribe Sánchez.name: Jon Ander Oribe Sánchez
Jon Ander Oribe Sánchez.summary: Jon Ander Oribe Sánchez is the CTO at Quantum Mads. Jon Ander Oribe Sánchez is a software developer by profession with a BS in computer science and software development from the Universidad Isabel I, Burgos, Spain. As CTO of Quantum Mads — a Spanish startup pioneering the quantum transition in finance, logistics and cybersecurity — Oribe Sánchez is responsible for its respective cloud application architecture design, the development of a pipeline for testing upcoming updates and tools to monitor the progress, hiring and managing IT specialists, data scientists and engineers, as well as designing of the company’s project roadmap.

John Morton.name: John Morton
John Morton.summary: John Morton is the CTO at Quantum Motion. John Morton is the founder and CTO of Quantum Motion, a London-based startup developing a revolutionary technology platform based on a scalable array of qubits fabricated on silicon with a CMOS compatible process. Yet that’s not all, for there must be 48 hours in Morton’s day, as he is also the director and co-founder of another London startup, Phasecraft.

Douglas Birch.name: Douglas Birch
Douglas Birch.summary: Douglas Birch is the CTO at Quantum Trilogy. Birch received an MSc in technology and innovation management from the University of Tampa.

Shahryar Shaghaghi.name: Shahryar Shaghaghi
Shahryar Shaghaghi.summary: Shahryar Shaghaghi is the CTO at Quantum Xchange. As CTO of Quantum Xchange, a leader in quantum-safe products and services for crypto agility and quantum readiness headquartered in Bethesda, Maryland, Shahryar Shaghaghi brings more than 30 years of experience in technology and risk management consulting, solutions/frameworks development, and global implementations to the position.

Duncan Earl.name: Duncan Earl
Duncan Earl.summary: Duncan Earl is the CTO at Qubitekk. Duncan Earl is founder, president & CTO of Qubitekk, a California-based startup developing components and systems for the emerging quantum sensing, quantum cryptography, and quantum computing markets. With more than two decades’ experience researching and building optical technologies for both the private sector and government organizations, Earl is an important cog in the Qubitekk machine.

George Siopsis.name: George Siopsis
George Siopsis.summary: George Siopsis is the CTO at Qubit Engineering. Theoretical particle physicist George Siopsis is co-founder and CTO of Qubit Engineering, a QC startup based in Tennessee that has developed new optimization methods for micro-siting for wind turbines. Along with his work at Qubit Engineering, Siopsis is a professor of physics at the University of Tennessee, currently working on quantum computing and quantum information processing, and along with collaborators at ORNL, he has built a quantum optics laboratory for quantum cryptography.

John Leiseboer.name: John Leiseboer
John Leiseboer.summary: John Leiseboer is the CTO at QuintessenceLabs. John Leiseboer is the CTO of QuintessenceLabs, an Australian quantum-enhanced cybersecurity startup based in Canberra, the nation’s capital. With over forty years developing commercial and military communications and security products, Leiseboer — whose specialties are in security architecture, encryption, key management, and data communications — brings his wealth of experience in technology and ideas to QuintessenceLabs’ task of delivering practical and secure communications products for its customer base.

Jelmer Renema.name: Jelmer Renema
Jelmer Renema.summary: Jelmer Renema is the CTO at QuiX. Jelmer Renema is the CTO of QuiX, a Netherlands-based startup providing a plug-and-play integrated and reconfigurable light-based quantum processor. A specialist in integrated photonics, quantum optics and superconducting devices, Renema has a Ph.D. from Leiden University in physics.

Mael Flament.name: Mael Flament
Mael Flament.summary: Mael Flament is the CTO at Qunnect. With more than a decade in the design of precision scientific instrumentation, before Qunnect Flament conducted research at Stony Brook University, Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, Brookhaven National Laboratory, CERN, and NASA. His work at Qunnect is, in his own words, “an extension of his graduate work developing a new generation of compact room-temperature quantum memories and quantum networking components in the Quantum Information Sciences and Technology laboratory at Stony Brook University.”.

Vivek Shenoy.name: Vivek Shenoy
Vivek Shenoy.summary: Vivek Shenoy is the CTO at QNu Labs. In the role since early 2021, Vivek Shenoy is the CTO of Indian startup QNu Labs, a Bengaluru-based enterprise that is leveraging quantum technology to offer solutions in data centre protection and multi-factor authentication. With experience as an R&D engineer, product architect and consultant, Shenoy employs his product design and pedagogy skills to disrupt the cybersecurity market on the subcontinent and beyond.

Kostantin Vilk.name: Kostantin Vilk
Kostantin Vilk.summary: Kostantin Vilk is the CTO at QuSecure. CTO of the founder of QuSecure, a San Mateo, California- based startup that has developed a quantum resilient technology to secure businesses from cyber attacks, Konstantin Vilk — a proven entrepreneur with two successful exits under his belt — has 20 years of senior leadership experience in cybersecurity, information security, product innovation, AI, QC, post-quantum cryptography, and other sectors. Vilk has a BA in business management from Monash University, Australia.

Oleg Mukhanov.name: Oleg Mukhanov
Oleg Mukhanov.summary: Oleg Mukhanov is the CTO at SeeQC. Oleg Mukhanov is the CTO and co-founder of SeeQC. Based in New York State, the startup is developing the first digital quantum computing platform for global businesses by combining classical and quantum technologies to address the efficiency, stability and cost issues endemic to quantum computing systems.

Robert Bedington.name: Robert Bedington
Robert Bedington.summary: Robert Bedington is the CTO at SpeQtral. CTO and co-founder of SpeQtral (formerly known as S15 Space Systems), Robert Bedington is hard at work developing commercial quantum secure communication from space, and laying the foundations for a global quantum internet.

Valerii Vinokur.name: Valerii Vinokur
Valerii Vinokur.summary: Valerii Vinokur is the CTO at Terra Quantum. In the job since early 2021, Valerii Vinokur is the CTO at Terra Quantum, a Swiss quantum cryptography company. One of the most highly decorated quantum scientists in the world who has made foundational contributions to our understanding of the physics of superconductors, Vinokur will oversee the project portfolio with a particular focus on hardware components, while applying his deep and long-term world-class experience to expanding the intellectual property and patent portfolio.

Yudong Cao.name: Yudong Cao
Yudong Cao.summary: Yudong Cao is the CTO at Zapata Computing. CTO and founding scientist of what is arguably the most high-profile QC startup on the list, the Boston, Massachusetts-based Zapata Computing, whose quantum platform Orquestra(R) combines a powerful software platform and quantum algorithm libraries to deliver real-world advances in computational power for applications. Have we missed any out or got something wrong?
